heading: Staff expenses for House Members attending organizational caucus or conference
label: 5343 Staff expenses for House Members attending organizational caucus or conference
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 43b–2 of this titleSection is based on section 1 of House Resolution No. 10, Ninety-fourth Congress, , which was enacted into permanent law by .Jan. 14, 1975Pub. L. 94–59

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Each Member-elect (other than an incumbent Member reelected to the ensuing Congress) who attends a caucus or conference called under , and each incumbent Member reelected to the ensuing Congress who attends any such caucus or conference convening after the adjournment sine die of the Congress in the year involved, shall be entitled to designate one staff person to be paid for one round trip between that person’s place of residence, provided such place of residence is in the district which the Member-elect or incumbent Member represents, and Washington, District of Columbia, for the purpose of accompanying that Member-elect or incumbent Member to such caucus or conference.section 29a(a) of this title

children.heading: Per diem expenses of staff person
children.label: b Per diem expenses of staff person
children.text: Each Member-elect (other than an incumbent Member reelected to the ensuing Congress) who attends a caucus or conference called under such shall be entitled to designate one staff person who shall in addition be reimbursed on a per diem or other basis for expenses incurred in accompanying the Member-elect at the time of such caucus or conference.section 29a(a) of this title

children.heading: Orientation programs for new Members
children.label: c Orientation programs for new Members
children.text: With the approval of the majority leader (in the case of a Member or Member-elect of the majority party) or the minority leader (in the case of a Member or Member-elect of the minority party), subsections (a) and (b) shall apply with respect to the attendance of a Member or Member-elect at a program conducted by the Committee on House Administration for the orientation of new members in the same manner as such provisions apply to the attendance of the Member or Member-elect at the organizational caucus or conference.1So in original. Probably should be capitalized.1
